For those of you who are not on certification yet, you're not seeing these. Sorry. Okay, it's only for those of you who are, all right? Have a great day and let's do some serious butt kicking on Monday. All right? Take care. We have two worlds that we live in. We have this inner world. It's because of sales and it's really critical when it comes to selling. We have this inner world of success. What happens up here between your ears before you ever go out into the world and take action and try to sell anybody anything? And there are certain distinct things that you need to know how to do pretty well if you want to succeed. If you can't do them, you can be the greatest sales person in the world with skills, but you're not going to succeed at a high level. So one of those things, by the way, and I'll do this out of order here, is your standards. Is what's your actual personal standard? What will you not sell for less than? Now, just so you know, most kids at 16, they made $125 in one day. They probably would have said, you know what? I'm going to go party for a few days. I'm going to go, why don't we go drink who knows what I'll do? And then when it runs out, I'll go, come on, no, no, not me. I had high financial standards. I wanted to be rich. I wanted money. I came from a poor family. My parents did not have any money in the family and they were both very, very smart people. They were both very educated people. Just to give you an idea of my mom, she's now 80 years old. At 64 years old, she went back to law school and passed the New York State bar at 68, the oldest woman in New York state to pass the bar. She's a very smart lady. Does all pro-bone work for women's charities. Before that, she was a CPA. Certainly we certified public accountants in the 50s when women were not in the workplace doing that. Very smart lady. My dad was a CPA. He was very smart. They were very educated. They were very hardworking. And they were very broke. They did not have money because number one, they worked with other people, but even more importantly, they did not like to sell. They thought influence was evil. So as much time as they had to do other people's books and to do others, they would not ever approach anybody. So I actually saw this in my house. Like, wow, I said, I came to this realization. I said, you can be really, really smart. You can be really, really educated. You can be really, really hardworking and be really, really broke at the same time. I got that. I said, there were other things missing. And one was that they hated selling. And the second was that my parents were completely called risk averse. They would not take any risks in life. They were from that oppression era mentality, where they, you know, hoarded their money, they didn't invest at all. And that's really cool, playing defense your whole life. They never played offense. And it's very hard to get ahead, especially in selling to salesperson playing defense your whole life. So my parents never had any money. They didn't have any money. They didn't have any money. And I went back to the world realizing that. And I was frustrated because we didn't have any money. And I saw my parents struggling. So I had these high financial standards. I wanted to go to college. So rather than just, you know, taking the money and partying for a day, the next day I went back with four pullers and I sold them all out. I made $500 the next day. That was a lot of money in 1978, $500 in a day in cash. That first summer I made $20,000. My first summer selling ISIS. I then did that every single summer and I then used that money to put myself through school, put myself through college that way. And I graduated college before I went out into the business world and staked my claim. I actually had one kick up, so to speak, on the road to entrepreneurial to sales success. And that was I spent one day in dental school. Believe it or not. I ain't getting this in the room. You don't fucking want to be a dentist. I mean, honestly, it's the best thing. It's the worst profession. I hope this is on Tent-C-C, by the way. But I mean, you gotta stick your hands in people's mouths. It's gross, right? But here's what happened. From the time I was 18 months old and I was in the high chair and my mom was, you know, giving me the bottle of the paparazzi in the apple sauce, from that first moment, as the food was going in, she was saying, the only way to become wealthy is you gotta be a doctor. You should have Jewish mother. You gotta be a doctor. The only normal wealth doctor. It's like fucking hypnosis, okay? And you gotta be a doctor. And you gotta be a doctor. And you gotta be a doctor. And you gotta be a doctor. And you gotta be a doctor. And you gotta be a doctor. And as the food was going in, she was saying, the only way to become wealthy is you gotta be a doctor. You should have Jewish mother. You gotta be a doctor. The only normal wealth doctor. It's like fucking hypnosis, okay? And it basically is, right? Hungry food, doctor, right? So what my mother was doing, just so you know, she was inserting a belief in me. And that makes up the second element of this inner game of success, the beliefs that we have. There are certain beliefs that we have, each of us. We all have them. That lead us to massive wealth and success itself. And life in general. And there are other beliefs that we have, which we call limiting beliefs. The first are our beliefs. The second set are limiting beliefs, and they hold us back from achieving. They stop us from getting what we deserve in life. And we all have them. These limiting beliefs, and this stuff, Father, there's a whole section on this in your workbook. And I'm not gonna go through this in that much detail today, because I want to focus on the actual hard core, the skill sets of selling. This stuff is in the workbook. It's powerful. It goes through a little bit. There's some exercise on what you do when you get home. It's really cool. I'm very angry. Today I want to focus on the real selling stuff. It's gonna help you make more money immediately. You know, starting tomorrow, right? So, but these belief systems that we have, imagine this, a limiting belief. It's almost like a governor on a Ferrari. Can I tell you this? Beautiful, no, half a million dollars, a chariot Ferrari. It's got 500 horsepower, beautiful chassis, 12 cylinders in the engine. People are doing 300 miles an hour. But there's a governor on the engine stopping the flow of gas. You're not going over 55. I don't care what the car is capable of doing. That one element holds it back and you lock go over 55. That is what the limiting belief does to you. It stops you from charging forward when you should. And it causes you to pull back when you should. An example would be what my parents had in their hearts and in their minds, which was selling is evil. Selling is manipulative. Let me tell you, selling is life. Either you're selling in life or you're failing.
